title: Eritrichium incanum
taxonomy: Eritrichium incanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It is a member of the genus Eritrichium, which contains several species of plants commonly known as woolly or downy louseworts. Eritrichium incanum is further divided into two subspecies: E. i. incanum and E. i. nevadense. The classification of Eritrichium incanum reflects its unique characteristics and its relationships with other plants in the Boraginaceae family.

intro: Eritrichium incanum, also known as White Fleabane or Hoary Fleabane, is a perennial plant species in the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial in treating various health issues, such as wounds, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Eritrichium incanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eritrichium incanum is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits.
